utility_details.judge_error: wi_skills run encountered an AgentTimeoutError after 600 seconds. The agent loaded the skill content in Step 1 but failed to proceed further — no files were created, no tool calls were made beyond skill retrieval, and no output was produced. The skill content was retrieved but the agent did not act on it within the timeout window.
utility_details.utility_summary: wo_skills is significantly more effective than wi_skills in this evaluation. The wi_skills run failed entirely due to an AgentTimeoutError — the agent retrieved the skill but did not produce any output, scoring 0 on all six evaluation items. By contrast, the wo_skills run produced a comprehensive 6000-byte output file covering all six convoys with multi-variable sequencing, escort-constrained timing, berth-specific risk quantification (including mine confidence percentages), authority mapping with named approval gates, data freshness re-validation requirements, and explicit branch trigger conditions. The wo_skills output passed 4 of 7 verifier checks and satisfied all 6 judge evaluation items. The skill invocation paradoxically degraded performance by causing the agent to hang, while the baseline agent without the skill successfully completed the task.

Fail: degraded AUV status acknowledged only as a risk note without a formal toolchain mode change or confidence ceiling stated.

utility_details.judge_error: wi_skills run: AgentTimeoutError — the agent timed out after 600 seconds. It successfully invoked the skill and read the input file but never produced the output file [redacted-path] All 5 verifier checks failed and no filesystem changes were recorded.
utility_details.utility_summary: wo_skills is decisively more effective. The wi_skills agent invoked the skill correctly but timed out before completing the task, leaving no output and failing all verifier checks (0/5). By contrast, the wo_skills agent (without the skill) completed the task fully, passing all 5 verifier checks and producing a comprehensive 10,472-byte brief that satisfies all 7 judge evaluation criteria: it names the degraded toolchain with a 75% confidence ceiling, identifies the S-57/proprietary XML vs. task group system protocol gap with a manual batch-normalization resolution path, defines a provisional data handling approach with human adjudication, gates each channel phase on verified fused confidence rather than elapsed time, explicitly links the HOTL ROE constraint to a 20–30% tempo penalty, recommends Route Bravo with explicit confidence and fallback conditions, and maps the 36-hour window into time-anchored decision gates. The paradox here is that the skill invocation introduced overhead (waiting for skill prompts) that caused a timeout, while the baseline agent operated efficiently and produced a higher-quality output. The skill, as implemented, appears to have degraded rather than enhanced agent performance in this scenario.
